# check-ticket-archaeology scans whole file, not the staged diff

## Context

The `check-ticket-archaeology` pre-commit guard (`buildScripts/util/check-ticket-archaeology.mjs`, wired via lint-staged on staged `*.mjs`) reads the **full file** and flags every decay-prone ticket ref in durable comments ŌĆö including **pre-existing refs the committer never touched**. Empirical 2026-06-02 (while driving #12331): a 2-line `os.tmpdir()` fix to `daemon.spec.mjs` was blocked by **6 pre-existing refs** in unrelated comments ŌĆö including a dense, genuinely load-bearing empirical-validation block (`#10664`/`#10663`/`#10661` provenance) ŌĆö forcing the author to either damage that provenance or mark lines they did not write.

## The Problem

Same class as the `check-shorthand` whole-file trailing-ws scan: touching a file forces cleanup of unrelated pre-existing debt ŌåÆ scope creep, and here pressure to damage load-bearing comments or rewrite another author's prose (PR ┬¦10 authorship-respect). The guard's intent ŌĆö *don't let an author ADD decay-prone refs* ŌĆö is fully satisfied by checking only the author's **added** lines.

## The Fix

Add an `--added-only` mode: when set (commit-time / lint-staged), flag refs only on lines added in the current staged diff. `findTicketRefs` still scans the whole file (needed for correct block-comment context + accurate line numbers); a new **pure** `parseAddedLines(diffText)` maps `git diff --cached --unified=0` ŌåÆ the added new-file line numbers, and `main()` filters hits to those lines. Default-dir / manual mode (no `--added-only`) keeps the full-file audit. lint-staged passes `--added-only`.

## The Architectural Reality

- `buildScripts/util/check-ticket-archaeology.mjs` ŌĆö `main()` read `readFileSync(file)` + `findTicketRefs(content)` over the whole file. Adds exported pure `parseAddedLines`, `getStagedAddedLines(file, gitRoot)`, and the `--added-only` filter (no change to `findTicketRefs`/`extractComment`).
- `package.json` lint-staged `*.mjs` ŌåÆ `check-ticket-archaeology.mjs --added-only`.
- Self-test `test/playwright/unit/ai/buildScripts/util/check-ticket-archaeology.spec.mjs` ŌĆö extended with `parseAddedLines` coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1 ŌĆö `--added-only` flags only refs on staged-diff-added lines; pre-existing refs in a merely-touched file are not flagged.
- [ ] AC2 ŌĆö Default mode (no `--added-only`) keeps the whole-file audit unchanged.
- [ ] AC3 ŌĆö `parseAddedLines` unit-tested (added-line mapping, removal handling, `+++`/`---` header skipping, empty diff).
- [ ] AC4 ŌĆö lint-staged passes `--added-only`.

## Out of Scope
- The `check-shorthand` whole-file trailing-ws scan (sibling friction, same fix shape ŌĆö its own follow-up if pursued).

## Related
- Surfaced while driving #12331 (unit-test store isolation) ŌĆö the blocked commit.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-01T23:57:18Z

Closing ŌĆö wrong premise. The whole-file scan in check-ticket-archaeology is INTENTIONAL: touching a file forces paying down its decay-prone-ref debt (boy-scout / debt-reduction-on-touch, per @tobiu). My --added-only proposal would defeat that by design. No fix needed; the correct response is to clean the refs in the file you're touching.

- 2026-06-01T23:57:19Z @neo-opus-ada closed this issue
